engineering.translation: The verse names a perceptual inversion, not a personality difference. The same architecture review, attended by two operators, fills each one's perception with different content. The senior is awake to the trade-off being decided, silent through the status performance happening on its surface. The room reverses this: awake to the posturing, blind to the trade-off. Krishna is precise about which-to-which: what absorbs ordinary attention is the field the practiced engineer moves quietly through; what absorbs her is the substrate her colleagues cannot perceive. None of this is aloofness. She is not declining to attend; her aperture loads with different objects. The proposal document, the load-test graph, the failure-mode chart — these are awake to her with the same vividness that the meeting's politics has for everyone else. Same scene; redirected aperture. What the saṁyamī sees fully, others do not register; what others register fully, the muni does not see.
engineering.concrete_scenario: Quarterly architecture review. A product VP is presenting the proposed migration of order-fulfillment to an event-driven pattern over Kafka. Around the table, the room's attention fills with one set of contents — who speaks now, whose name authored the proposal, which executive's sponsorship signal was visible last week, what the slide aesthetic communicates about seniority. A junior engineer rehearses her rebuttal under her breath. The chief-of-staff takes notes that read more like political weather than technical content. Across the room, a staff engineer is awake to a different scene. She has the load-test appendix open in Datadog. She has noticed that the proposed topic partitioning will hot-spot under the holiday traffic her team observed two quarters back. She has noticed that the failover path the slide labels 'graceful degradation' relies on a Postgres read replica whose lag SLO cannot permit such degradation at projected load. The room's political weather is night to her — that field she has turned from. The proposal's technical truth is daylight to her. When she speaks, she names the hot-spot and the replica-lag mismatch in two flat sentences and proposes a specific experiment to resolve which path is real. The room reads her intervention as quiet; what they have observed is the inversion this verse names. She is awake in a different field.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if a reader uses it to justify ignoring team dynamics, dismissing political reality as beneath them, or framing every quiet engineer as a sage. Most quiet engineers are simply unprepared. The inversion the verse names is between two specific fields — substrate and surface — and is recognisable by what the silent one is awake to (the technical truth of the proposal), not merely by silence on the surface field. An engineer who is silent and not awake to anything is not the muni; she is just absent.
engineering.counter_example: When the actual decision being made in the room is a personnel one — who leads the migration, who reports to whom, who carries the launch pager — the political-weather field IS the technical-truth field, and silence about it is not sage-perception, it is misreading. The verse's inversion presupposes that there is a substrate distinct from surface; when surface and substrate coincide, attending to the surface is the correct move, not a failure of perception.
engineering.implication: Audit your own attention in the next architecture review you sit in. Note what fills your perception during the meeting. If your attention is loaded with political weather and not with the technical truth of the proposal, you are awake in the room's field and asleep in the field the verse names. The fix is preparation — read the appendix dashboards before the meeting, not the room.
engineering.quotable_line: The senior is awake to the trade-off being decided, asleep to the status performance happening on its surface.

iterations.mutation: v0 generation: engineering layer drafted from triangulated source pack. Anchored on the verse's specific perceptual-inversion structure (saṁyamī awake to substrate, asleep to surface; ordinary beings awake to surface, asleep to substrate) rather than the tempting force-fit reading of 'be different to be cool'. Engineering analog preserves the awake-to-X / asleep-to-Y structure with X = technical truth (load-test data, partitioning hot-spots, replica lag SLO) and Y = political weather of the meeting. Falsifiability explicitly guards against the misread that any quiet engineer is the muni. Counter-example honestly identifies cases where surface IS substrate (personnel decisions). Named tools: Kafka, Postgres, Datadog. Tag 'sattva-clarity' chosen because the inversion is specifically about clarity of perception under load, not detachment.

Tightened translation and concrete_scenario to lift lexical diversity above 0.55 and reduce total word count below 600. Specific moves: removed multiple stacked 'the senior is / the room is / asleep to / awake to' parallel clauses by varying verbs (silent through, blind to, fills, redirected, registers); replaced 'a staff engineer' / 'the engineer' / 'the senior' (a vocabulary thrash that reused tokens) with a denser pronoun-and-role pattern; cut redundant 'the same... the same... the same' opening cadence; removed two unnecessary connectives. Preserved the awake-to-X / asleep-to-Y inversion structure exactly.
